publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:44:55 UTC.

ead.archdesc:
======= RMM08791Larry Fink photographs8791Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections2B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:14:29 UTC. >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Larry Fink photographs
Davis, Gary S.
8791
.6 cubic feet. 30 gelatin silver prints
1960-2004 30 photographs by Larry Fink featuring Jazz musicians. 15294704
Content Description
Thirty (30) photographs by Larry Fink featuring Jazz musicians. Several of the photos were part of his Somewhere There's Music project (published 2011). Musicians include Jason Moran, John Coltrane, Albert Ayler, Don Ayler, Archie Shepp, Dave Burrell, Leroy Jenkins, Jackie McLean, and others. Venues include The Village Vanguard and the Jazz Arts Society.
Biographical / Historical
Larry Fink was born in Brooklyn in 1941. He is an American photographer best known for his black and white images depicting people in social situations.
Preferred Citation
Larry Fink photographs, #8791. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
African American musicians African Americans -- Music Jazz. Jazz musicians -- United States. Photographers. Photographs Documentary photography Fink, Larry, 1941-
